title: DeepSeek Non-Native Tool-Call Normalization Research Package

issue.symptom: DeepSeek API rejects a VS Code extension request with HTTP 400 because a message content field is not a string or list. The visible model output includes a fallback tool call in bare XML form: <manage_todo_list>{...}</manage_todo_list>.
likely_failure_modes: Non-native DeepSeek fallback models are receiving OpenAI-native tool_call message parts.
At least one message may be serialized with content: null.
Streaming text is reported before the fallback parser suppresses and dispatches tool calls.
The fallback parser does not yet normalize bare XML tags where the tag name is the tool name.

mcp_gateway.note: FastMCP Client("http://localhost:8087/mcp") failed in Codex with Session terminated.

arguments.query: LLM tool call parser normalize JSON XML function-call streaming tool result
parser state machine streaming parser tool invocation normalize structured output
tool call parser JSON XML fallback parser malformed model output
agent tool calling loop parser dispatch tool result flow
parser state machine recursive descent parsing structured output validation JSON XML tool calls

cre_code_chunks.relevant_pattern: A cumulative JSON output parser strips model text, supports partial parsing during streaming, returns null-like absence on incomplete JSON, and raises parser-specific errors only for final invalid JSON.
cre_code_chunks.short_excerpt: Parse the output of an LLM call to a JSON object.
cre_code_chunks.application: Use partial parse semantics while streaming, and only hard-fail once the final chunk has arrived.

cre_code_chunks.relevant_pattern: XML parsing is treated as model-output parsing, not transport truth. Expected tags are advisory; implementations still need robust extraction and error handling.
cre_code_chunks.short_excerpt: Parse an output using xml format.
cre_code_chunks.application: For fallback XML tool calls, parse only after isolation of a complete candidate tag, then validate against the extension's known tool registry.

cre_code_chunks.relevant_pattern: A streaming parser interface accepts chunks incrementally and returns user-visible content separately from thinking text and tool calls.
cre_code_chunks.short_excerpt: Add processes streamed content and returns parsed content, thinking, and tool calls
cre_code_chunks.application: The VS Code extension should buffer possible tool-call spans and only report ordinary text after parser classification.

cre_code_chunks.relevant_pattern: The parser wraps raw model text in a synthetic root XML element, unmarshals it, trims and validates the function name, pairs argument keys and values, finds the matching tool schema, then coerces values by parameter type.
cre_code_chunks.short_excerpt: Wrap the content in a root element to make it valid XML
cre_code_chunks.application: Bare <manage_todo_list>...</manage_todo_list> should be normalized through a wrapper/root parse step, then checked against known tools before dispatch.

cre_code_chunks.relevant_pattern: The XML tool-call parser is coupled with partial JSON and partial regex helpers, implying fallback tool parsing needs incomplete-input handling.
cre_code_chunks.application: Treat fallback tool-call detection as a streaming parse problem, not a final-response regex-only problem.

cre_code_chunks.relevant_pattern: A small explicit state machine tracks object, field, value, and post-value states while preserving token positions.
cre_code_chunks.application: Use explicit parser states for fallback text: outside text, possible tag, inside tool args, complete tool, malformed.

textbook_passages.passage_note: The chapter frames parsing as recognizing a stream of tokens against a grammar, building structure from terminals and nonterminals.
textbook_passages.short_passage_excerpt: it sees a stream of words
textbook_passages.application: Fallback LLM output can be handled as a token stream where text, tag delimiters, tool names, and JSON payloads are grammar terminals.

textbook_passages.book_title: Compilers Principles Techniques and Tools 2nd Edition
textbook_passages.passage_note: The chapter connects recursive-descent procedures with lookahead-based decisions and match routines.
textbook_passages.application: The fallback parser should use lookahead before emitting text, because a '<' may begin either literal content or a tool-call tag.

textbook_passages.book_title: Compilers Principles Techniques and Tools 2nd Edition
textbook_passages.passage_note: The LR parser material emphasizes explicit action decisions over an input buffer and stack.
textbook_passages.application: A fallback streaming parser should delay output until it can shift/reduce a full candidate tool call or safely classify the bytes as plain text.

textbook_passages.passage_note: The chapter's parser loops while the next token extends the current expression, then exits when no extension applies.
textbook_passages.application: Fallback tool parsing can use the same loop shape: keep consuming while a known tool-call continuation is present; otherwise flush normal text.

textbook_passages.passage_note: The chapter result emphasizes parser tests around concrete sample input.
textbook_passages.application: Add regression tests for exact DeepSeek fallback strings, including bare XML tag tool calls and malformed JSON.

textbook_code_blocks.book_title: Compilers Principles Techniques and Tools 2nd Edition
short_blocks: void optexprO { if ( lookahead == expr ) match(expr);
textbook_code_blocks.application: Use a small deterministic dispatcher keyed by tool-name lookahead, not a provider-specific payload assumption.

textbook_code_blocks.book_title: Compilers Principles Techniques and Tools 2nd Edition
short_blocks: } else if ( ACTION[S, a] = reduce A -> beta ) {
textbook_code_blocks.application: Model streaming chunks as an input buffer with explicit shift/reduce decisions.
Continue reading until the candidate call is complete; do not emit partial tool-call text.

short_blocks: context-free grammar G is a quadruple (T, NT, S, P)
textbook_code_blocks.application: Define the fallback protocol grammar explicitly: tool tag, JSON object body, closing tag, optional surrounding text.

diagrams.short_excerpt: expression grammar over variables and operators
diagrams.application: Useful as a grammar-design reference for expressing fallback tool-call syntax.

diagrams.book_title: Systems Performance Enterprise and the Cloud 2nd Edition
diagrams.application: Demonstrates that diagram_search was operational, but this hit is not directly useful for LLM tool parsing.
Weakly related to event/branch selection; not enough to drive implementation.

diagrams.book_title: Compilers Principles Techniques and Tools 2nd Edition
diagrams.application: Confirms diagram index access, but not a primary source for this fix.

implementation_recommendation.summary: Normalize by capability. Native providers can receive OpenAI-style tool_calls. Non-native fallback providers should receive text-only prompts and parse model-produced text into internal tool calls.
steps: Create a provider capability gate before request serialization.
For DeepSeek fallback mode, map message content to string or list only; never send content: null.
Strip or translate VS Code LanguageModelToolCallPart and tool-result parts before sending to DeepSeek if the model is configured as non-native.
Add a fallback parser arm for bare XML tool tags: <toolName>{json}</toolName>.
Validate toolName against registered tools and validate JSON args against the tool schema.
In streaming mode, buffer possible tool-call spans and suppress raw fallback markup until classified.
Add tests for complete tool call, partial streaming chunks, malformed JSON, unknown tool, and surrounding normal text.
